Carrier.E1: Low pressure fault — Check refrigerant charge, indoor filter, evaporator coil airflow
Carrier.E2: High pressure fault — Check condenser coil cleanliness, outdoor fan motor, refrigerant overcharge
Carrier.E3: Compressor overcurrent — Check amp draw, capacitor, wiring connections
Carrier.E4: Discharge temperature too high — Check refrigerant charge, expansion valve, airflow
Carrier.E5: Communication error between indoor and outdoor — Check wiring, control board connections
Carrier.E6: Indoor fan motor fault — Check motor windings, capacitor, control board relay
Carrier.E7: Outdoor fan motor fault — Check motor windings, capacitor, contactor
Carrier.E8: Phase protection (3-phase) — Check phase sequence, voltage on all legs
Carrier.E9: Refrigerant flow error — Check TXV, filter drier, refrigerant charge
Carrier.13: Limit circuit lockout — Check high-limit switch, airflow, gas valve
Carrier.14: Ignition lockout — Check igniter, gas valve, flame sensor
Carrier.21: Gas heating lockout — Check gas supply pressure, gas valve, igniter
Carrier.22: Abnormal flame signal — Check flame sensor rod, ground, gas valve leakthrough
Carrier.23: Pressure switch did not close — Check inducer motor, flue blockage, pressure switch hose
Carrier.24: Secondary voltage fuse blown — Check for short in 24V wiring, transformer
Carrier.31: High-pressure switch open — Check condenser airflow, refrigerant charge, outdoor fan
Carrier.32: Low-pressure switch open — Check refrigerant charge, filter drier, TXV
Carrier.33: Rollout switch open — Check for cracked heat exchanger, gas valve, airflow
Carrier.34: Ignition signal timeout — Check flame sensor, gas valve, ignition module
Carrier.41: Blower motor fault — Check motor, capacitor, control board
Carrier.42: Indoor coil thermistor open/short — Check thermistor wiring, replace sensor
Carrier.44: Outdoor coil thermistor open/short — Check thermistor wiring, replace sensor
Carrier.45: Outdoor ambient thermistor fault — Check sensor wiring, replace sensor
Carrier.46: Discharge line thermistor fault — Check sensor connection, replace sensor

Trane.126: Low pressure fault — Check refrigerant charge, indoor coil, TXV
Trane.127: High pressure fault — Check condenser coil, outdoor fan, overcharge
Trane.FP: Fuse protection — Check control board fuse, 24V circuit wiring
Trane.AL: Analog limit exceeded — Check sensor readings, calibration
Trane.CO: Communication fault — Check thermostat wiring, dip switches, communication bus
Trane.E1: Compressor overcurrent — Check compressor amps, capacitor, contactor
Trane.E2: Indoor fan fault — Check ECM motor, wiring, control board
Trane.E3: High discharge temp — Check superheat, charge, airflow restriction
Trane.E4: Low suction pressure — Check charge, filter drier, TXV
Trane.E5: High suction pressure — Check compressor valves, overcharge
Trane.E6: Coil freeze protection — Check airflow, filter, charge, blower speed
Trane.E7: Phase loss or reversal — Check 3-phase power supply, fuses
Trane.E8: Compressor thermal overload — Check ambient temp, charge, contactor
Trane.E9: Low oil pressure — Check oil level, oil pump, compressor bearings
Trane.HI: Ignition lockout — Check igniter (HSI), flame sensor, gas valve
Trane.HL: High-limit switch open — Check airflow, filter, blower motor, ductwork
Trane.LP: Low-pressure switch open — Check charge, filter drier, airflow
Trane.HP: High-pressure switch open — Check condenser, fan motor, charge
Trane.PS: Pressure switch fault — Check inducer motor, flue, drain trap, pressure switch tubing
Trane.FS: Flame sense fault — Check flame rod microamp reading (should be >1.0 µA), ground
Trane.GV: Gas valve fault — Check gas valve operation, 24V signal, gas supply
Trane.IF: Indoor fan fault — Check ECM module, motor, wiring harness
Trane.OF: Outdoor fan fault — Check fan motor, capacitor, relay

Lennox.E1: Compressor fault — Check start components, wiring, control board relay
Lennox.E2: Outdoor coil sensor fault — Check thermistor, wiring connector
Lennox.E3: Indoor coil sensor fault — Check thermistor, wiring connector
Lennox.E4: Outdoor ambient sensor fault — Check sensor resistance, wiring
Lennox.E5: Communication error — Check wiring between indoor/outdoor units, control boards
Lennox.E6: Compressor overcurrent — Check compressor windings, capacitor, contactor
Lennox.E7: High discharge temp — Check charge, TXV, airflow
Lennox.E8: Fan motor fault — Check motor, capacitor, control board output
Lennox.E9: Low pressure protection — Check charge, TXV, indoor coil
Lennox.E10: High pressure protection — Check outdoor coil, fan motor, overcharge
Lennox.201: Pressure switch stuck closed — Check pressure switch, tubing
Lennox.202: Pressure switch stuck open — Check inducer motor, flue blockage, condensate drain
Lennox.203: Limit switch open — Check airflow, filter, ductwork, blower motor
Lennox.204: Ignition fault — Check HSI igniter resistance (40-200 ohms typical), gas valve
Lennox.205: Flame lost during run — Check flame sensor (clean with scotch-brite), gas pressure
Lennox.207: Rollout switch open — Check heat exchanger, burner alignment, airflow
Lennox.208: Twinning communication fault — Check twinning cable, board configuration
Lennox.210: Blower motor fault — Check motor, module, wiring
Lennox.211: System configuration error — Check dip switch settings, jumpers
Lennox.270: Condensate overflow — Check drain trap, drain line, condensate pump
Lennox.281: Low stage pressure switch — Check inducer operation on low fire
Lennox.292: Gas valve circuit fault — Check gas valve coil resistance, wiring

Goodman.E1: Low pressure fault — Check refrigerant charge, filter drier, TXV
Goodman.E2: High pressure fault — Check condenser, outdoor fan, charge
Goodman.E3: Compressor overcurrent — Check amps, start components, wiring
Goodman.E4: Discharge temp high — Check superheat, charge, restriction
Goodman.E5: Communication fault — Check control wire, signal connections
Goodman.E6: Indoor fan fault — Check blower motor, capacitor, control board
Goodman.E7: Outdoor fan fault — Check fan motor, capacitor, contactor relay
Goodman.E8: Inverter module fault — Check drive board, power supply, wiring
Goodman.1F: Low-speed blower fault — Check motor, speed tap, board
Goodman.2H: High-limit switch open — Check airflow, filter, blower operation
Goodman.2L: Rollout switch open — Check heat exchanger integrity, burner operation
Goodman.3H: Pressure switch stuck closed — Check for water in hose, faulty switch
Goodman.3L: Pressure switch open — Check inducer motor, condensate trap, flue restriction
Goodman.4H: Ignition lockout (3 tries) — Check HSI igniter, gas valve, gas supply
Goodman.4L: Flame lost — Check flame sensor microamps (>0.5 µA), gas pressure, flame rod
Goodman.5H: Gas valve relay energized with no call — Check control board, wiring
Goodman.6H: Low line voltage — Check incoming power, transformer, wiring
Goodman.6L: Reversed polarity — Check L1/neutral wiring at disconnect and panel
Goodman.7H: Inducer motor fault — Check motor, wiring, control board output
Goodman.GS: Gas supply fault — Check gas pressure at manifold (3.5 in WC natural gas)

Rheem.E1: Open high-temperature limit — Check airflow, filter, blower operation
Rheem.E2: Rollout switch open — Check heat exchanger, gas pressure, airflow
Rheem.E3: Pressure switch stuck closed — Check switch, tubing, inducer
Rheem.E4: Pressure switch open — Check inducer motor, condensate drain, flue blockage
Rheem.E5: Flame sensed with no call — Check gas valve seat, flame sensor wiring
Rheem.E6: Ignition lockout — Check igniter, gas valve, gas supply pressure
Rheem.E7: Flame lost during run — Check flame sensor, gas pressure, wind conditions
Rheem.E8: Check polarity or grounding — Verify L1 on hot, proper ground
Rheem.E9: Low voltage — Check 24V transformer, thermostat wiring
Rheem.C1: Low pressure switch open — Check refrigerant charge, airflow
Rheem.C2: High pressure switch open — Check condenser, fan, charge
Rheem.C3: Compressor thermal overload — Check amp draw, ambient, charge
Rheem.C4: Outdoor fan motor fault — Check motor, capacitor, relay
Rheem.C5: Outdoor coil sensor fault — Check thermistor, connector
Rheem.C6: Indoor coil sensor fault — Check thermistor, connector
Rheem.C7: Communication error — Check wiring, control boards
Rheem.C8: Compressor discharge temp fault — Check charge, TXV, airflow
Rheem.C9: Inverter overcurrent — Check compressor windings, drive board
Rheem.dF: Defrost fault — Check defrost board, outdoor coil sensor, reversing valve
Rheem.FS: Flame sensor fault — Clean sensor, check microamps (need >1.0 µA)

York.E1: Indoor coil temp sensor fault — Check thermistor resistance, wiring
York.E2: Outdoor coil temp sensor fault — Check thermistor resistance, wiring
York.E3: Compressor discharge sensor fault — Check sensor, wiring
York.E4: Outdoor ambient sensor fault — Check sensor, wiring
York.E5: Communication fault — Check signal wire, connections, control boards
York.E6: Compressor overcurrent — Check windings, start components, amp draw
York.E7: High discharge temperature — Check charge, TXV, superheat
York.E8: PFC circuit fault — Check power factor correction board, incoming power
York.E9: Inverter IPM fault — Check drive board, power module, DC bus voltage
York.EA: Compressor phase fault — Check winding resistance (should be balanced ±0.5 ohm)
York.F1: High pressure protection — Check condenser, fan, charge
York.F2: Low pressure protection — Check charge, TXV, indoor coil
York.F3: Compressor overcurrent (phase) — Check contactor, wiring, power supply
York.F4: Fan motor fault — Check motor, capacitor, control output
York.F6: Outdoor fan fault — Check fan blade, motor, bearing
York.F7: AC voltage abnormal — Check incoming voltage at disconnect
York.F8: DC bus fault — Check drive board, capacitors, rectifier
York.14: Ignition lockout — Check igniter, flame sensor, gas valve
York.23: Pressure switch fault — Check inducer, drain trap, flue
York.24: Limit switch open — Check airflow, filter, blower motor

Daikin.A1: Indoor PCB fault — Check control board, reset power
Daikin.A3: Condensate drain level high — Check drain line, pump, float switch
Daikin.A5: Freeze/overheat protection — Check indoor coil temp, airflow, charge
Daikin.A6: Indoor fan motor fault — Check motor, wiring, PCB output
Daikin.A7: Swing motor fault — Check louver motor, drive circuit
Daikin.C4: Indoor coil thermistor fault — Check thermistor resistance (10k at 77°F typical)
Daikin.C9: Indoor ambient thermistor fault — Check sensor, wiring
Daikin.E1: Outdoor PCB fault — Check board, power supply, reset
Daikin.E3: High pressure switch activated — Check condenser, fan, charge
Daikin.E4: Low pressure switch activated — Check charge, TXV, filter drier
Daikin.E5: Compressor motor overload — Check amp draw, charge, wiring
Daikin.E6: Compressor motor locked/overcurrent — Check start components, windings
Daikin.E7: Outdoor fan motor fault — Check motor, capacitor, PCB relay
Daikin.E9: Electronic expansion valve fault — Check EEV coil, wiring, PCB
Daikin.F3: Discharge pipe temp too high — Check charge, indoor airflow, TXV
Daikin.F6: Abnormal high pressure (sensor) — Check transducer, charge
Daikin.H6: Position detection fault (compressor) — Check wiring, inverter board
Daikin.H8: DC input current fault — Check power supply, compressor
Daikin.J3: Discharge pipe thermistor fault — Check sensor resistance, connector
Daikin.J6: Outdoor heat exchanger thermistor fault — Check sensor, wiring
Daikin.L3: Electrical parts box temp high — Check ventilation, ambient
Daikin.L4: Inverter heat sink temp high — Check heat sink fan, ambient temp
Daikin.U0: Low refrigerant / electronic expansion valve fault — Check charge and EEV
Daikin.U4: Communication error (indoor/outdoor) — Check signal wiring, PCB connections, power

Mitsubishi.E0: Remote controller signal error — Check remote batteries, signal receiver
Mitsubishi.E1: Remote controller communication error — Check wiring, receiver board
Mitsubishi.E3: No return signal from indoor — Check indoor unit PCB, wiring
Mitsubishi.E6: Indoor/outdoor communication error — Check signal wire (S-wire), connections
Mitsubishi.E7: Outdoor communication error (multi) — Check branch box wiring
Mitsubishi.E9: Indoor/outdoor mismatch — Check model compatibility, configuration
Mitsubishi.F1: Indoor thermistor fault (room temp) — Check sensor, connector
Mitsubishi.F2: Indoor coil thermistor fault — Check sensor resistance, wiring
Mitsubishi.F3: Outdoor condenser thermistor fault — Check sensor, connector
Mitsubishi.F4: Outdoor ambient thermistor fault — Check sensor
Mitsubishi.F5: Outdoor compressor discharge thermistor — Check sensor wiring
Mitsubishi.F6: Outdoor coil thermistor (2nd) fault — Check sensor
Mitsubishi.L3: Electrical box temp rise — Check heat sink, ventilation, ambient
Mitsubishi.L4: Inverter heat sink overheat — Check fan, ambient, charge
Mitsubishi.P1: Intake sensor error (variable speed) — Check board sensor input
Mitsubishi.P2: Indoor coil sensor fault — Check thermistor wiring
Mitsubishi.P4: Drain sensor / float switch fault — Check drain line, pump, float
Mitsubishi.P5: Condensate pump fault — Check pump motor, float switch, wiring
Mitsubishi.P6: Freeze/overheat protection — Check airflow, charge, coil temp

Mitsubishi.U2: Power supply fault (undervoltage/overvoltage) — Check incoming voltage
Mitsubishi.U3: No start signal — Check thermostat, control wiring
Mitsubishi.U4: Indoor/outdoor communication fault — Check transmission wiring, boards
Mitsubishi.U5: Abnormal condenser temp — Check outdoor coil, fan motor, charge
